title: Palitana temples
tagline: Palitana temples — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: The Palitana temples, often known only as Palitana, are a large complex of Jain temples located on Shatrunjaya hills near Palitana in Bhavnagar district, Gujarat, India.
overview.description: The Palitana temples, often known only as Palitana, are a large complex of Jain temples located on Shatrunjaya hills near Palitana in Bhavnagar district, Gujarat, India. Also known as "Padliptapur of Kathiawad" in historic texts, the dense collection of almost 900 small shrines and large temples have led many to call Palitana the "city of temples". It is one of the most sacred sites of the Śvetāmbara tradition within Jainism. The earliest temples in the complex date as far back as the 11th century CE. The Palitana temple complex is near the top of the hill, in groups called Tonks (Tuks) along

topPlaces.name: Shatrunjaya
topPlaces.description: Shatrunjaya, also spelled Shetrunjaya ("place of victory against inner enemies") and originally known as Pundarikgiri, is a range of hills located near the city of Palitana in the Bhavnagar district of Gujarat, India. The hills are situated on the banks of the Shetrunji River at an elevation of approximately 164 feet (50 m) above sea level.

topPlaces.name: Palitana State
topPlaces.description: Palitana was a princely state in British India until 1948. The capital was the city of Palitana.

topPlaces.name: Palitana
topPlaces.description: Palitana is a town in the Bhavnagar district of the Indian state of Gujarat. It is one of the most significant pilgrimage destinations for followers of Jainism, renowned for the Shatrunjaya hill temples, a sprawling complex of over 900 marble temples considered among the holiest in Jainism.

topPlaces.name: Chok, Gujarat
topPlaces.description: Chok is a village in Jesar Taluka of Bhavnagar district, Gujarat, India. It is a former petty Rajput princely state.

items.note: Shatrunjaya, also spelled Shetrunjaya ("place of victory against inner enemies") and originally known as Pundarikgiri, is a range of hills located near the city of Palitana in the Bhavnagar district of Gujarat, India. The hills are situated on the banks of the Shetrunji River at an elevation of approximately 164 feet (50 m) above sea level.
Palitana was a princely state in British India until 1948. The capital was the city of Palitana.

Palitana is a town in the Bhavnagar district of the Indian state of Gujarat. It is one of the most significant pilgrimage destinations for followers of Jainism, renowned for the Shatrunjaya hill temples, a sprawling complex of over 900 marble temples considered among the holiest in Jainism.
Chok is a village in Jesar Taluka of Bhavnagar district, Gujarat, India. It is a former petty Rajput princely state.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Palitana temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Palitana temples is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Palitana temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bhavnagar Airport (50 km) and the nearest railway station is Surat Railway Station (113 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Palitana temples?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Palitana temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Palitana temples?
faq.a: Stays in Palitana temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Palitana temples?
faq.a: The top attractions are Shatrunjaya, Palitana State, Palitana, Chok, Gujarat.
